subject: Statistics
title: GPS and Wearable Data in Sports
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on GPS and Wearable Data in Sports.

hook.text: An NFL player's jersey hides a GPS tracker that logs every sprint, cut, and collision in real time.

buildup.text: Wearable devices track distance, speed, heart rate, and acceleration at 10 Hz — ten readings per second.

discovery.text: Coaches use the data to manage workload. A player running 10% more than usual might be headed for injury.

twist.text: Too much data creates noise. A coach can drown in metrics without knowing which ones actually predict injuries.

climax.text: The best teams use wearable data for load management — resting players before they break, not after.

punchline.text: Prevent injury before it happens. The data shows who's at risk.

quiz.question: How do teams use GPS wearable data for injury prevention?
options: They monitor workload and rest players who exceed their normal training load
They predict the exact date of future injuries
They only use it for post-injury rehabilitation
